title: CIL Geology — Advanced Mock 12 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.stem: The mean of 100 items is 50. If each item is increased by 5 and divided by 5, new mean is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A die is rolled twice. Probability of getting a doublet:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average weight of 8 persons increases by 2.5 kg when a new person replaces one weighing 65 kg. The new person's weight is:
questions.solution: 85 kg

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two vessels have milk:water 5:2 and 7:3. In what ratio mix to get 8:3?

questions.stem: Find the least number which when divided by 3, 5, 7, 9 leaves remainder 2 in each case:

questions.stem: How many numbers between 1000 and 9999 have no repeated digits?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In a class, 40% are boys. 60% of boys and 80% of girls passed. Random student passed, probability it's a girl:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A chord of a circle radius 10 cm subtends right angle at center. Minor segment area:
questions.solution: 28.5 cm²

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat goes 20 km downstream in 1 h. To return 20 km, it takes 1 h 20 min. Boat speed:
questions.solution: 25 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A works twice as fast as B. If both finish in 10 days, B alone:
questions.solution: 30 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If a × b = 240 and LCM(a, b) = 60, then HCF(a, b) is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A, B, C can do in 16, 24, 48 days. They work together. A leaves 4 days before completion. Total days:
questions.solution: 12.33 days

questions.stem: Amount doubles in 10 years at simple interest. Rate percent:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The curved surface area of a cylinder is 2640 cm² and radius 14 cm. Height:
questions.solution: 30 cm

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Profit earned by selling an article at ₹1,040 is 20% more than loss at ₹950. Cost price:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of people, distance between R and S from left is 8 and 14. T is 6th left of S. R's position if T is 2nd:
questions.solution: 12th from left

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: The company with the best safety record won the industry award. Conclusion I: The company has zero accidents. II: Safety is valued in the industry.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A company policy says employees with >5 years tenure get extra leave. Ram has 4 years 11 months experience. Is he eligible?
questions.solution: No

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km towards north, turns right 12 km, right 5 km, left 3 km. How far from start?
questions.solution: 15 km

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Between 2 and 3, when are hands at right angle?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A,B,C,D,E,F,G sit in circle. A is 2nd right of B. C is 3rd left of D. E is between F and G. F is opposite A. If B is left of E, who is opposite D?
questions.solution: B

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a pack of 52 cards arranged, the King of Hearts is 15th from top and 38th from bottom. Total cards?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: The stock market reached an all-time high this quarter. Conclusion I: The economy is performing well. II: All companies are profitable.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A college has 7 lectures, 3 tutorials, and 5 practical sessions. A student must attend at least 5 lectures and all practicals. Minimum sessions:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man faces west. Turns 135° clockwise, then 45° anticlockwise. Which direction now?
questions.solution: South

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: How many times do clock hands overlap in 12 hours?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Six friends sit in circle facing center. Rohan is between Mohan and Sohan. Tohan is opposite Rohan. Gohan is left of Tohan. Johan is right of Mohan. Who is between Sohan and Tohan?
questions.solution: Johan

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row facing north, P is 3rd from left. Q is 5th from right. If R is between P and Q and there are 2 people between Q and R. Total people?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: Several states have announced drought relief packages. Conclusion I: These states are facing drought. II: Central government will also announce relief.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: From 4 officers and 8 soldiers, a squad of 2 officers and 4 soldiers. A specific officer must be included. Number of squads:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Rohan walks 12 m north, 5 m east, 12 m south. Distance from start:
questions.solution: 5 m

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: ZA, YB, XC, WD, ?
questions.solution: VE

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: How many triangles can be formed using 6 non-collinear points?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, 1=A, 2=B, ..., 26=Z. MOBILE = 13-15-2-9-12-5. LAPTOP = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Oxygen : Combustion :: Hydrogen : ?
questions.solution: Combustion

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A ship sails 20 km east, 15 km north, 20 km west, 25 km south. Distance from start:
questions.solution: 10 km south

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: X and Y are brothers. Z is the daughter of Y. W is the son of X's sister. How is W related to Z?
questions.solution: Cousin

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, EARTH = 512018, SUN = 192114, then MOON = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: 2, 11, 38, 197, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of BENEVOLENT:
questions.solution: Cruel

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: safety / should / mines / our / be / in / priority / top
options: Safety in mines should be our top priority
In mines safety our top priority should be
Top priority safety should be in mines
Safety should be our top priority in mines
questions.solution: Safety should be our top priority in mines

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of RESILIENT:
questions.solution: Fragile

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: responsible / are / for / who / safety / miners / ensuring
options: Responsible for miners safety who is
Miners safety who is responsible for ensuring
Who is responsible for ensuring miners safety
Ensuring safety for miners who is responsible
questions.solution: Who is responsible for ensuring miners safety

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of BELLIGERENT:
questions.solution: Peaceful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: solve / team / the / to / problem / together / worked
options: The team worked together to solve the problem
Worked together the team to solve the problem
Together the team worked to solve the problem
To solve the problem the team together worked
questions.solution: The team worked together to solve the problem

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ALOOF:
questions.solution: Friendly

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: data / protect / essential / is / privacy / to
options: Essential is to protect data privacy
Data privacy is essential to protect
questions.solution: Protect data privacy is essential

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of OSTENTATIOUS:
questions.solution: Modest

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: environmental / concerns / need / planning / careful
questions.solution: Environmental concerns need careful planning

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of MAGNANIMOUS:
questions.solution: Stingy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: globalisation / economies / has / world / around / the / transformed
options: Around the world globalisation has transformed economies
Globalisation has transformed economies around the world
Transformed economies globalisation has around the world
Economies around the world has globalisation transformed
questions.solution: Globalisation has transformed economies around the world

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of DILIGENT:
questions.solution: Lazy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: modern / has / technology / efficiency / improved / significantly
options: Modern technology has improved efficiency significantly
Efficiency has modern technology improved significantly
Improved efficiency has significantly modern technology
Technology modern has efficiency improved significantly
questions.solution: Modern technology has improved efficiency significantly

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ABSTAIN:
questions.solution: Indulge

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: innovation / for / key / is / growth / economic
options: Innovation is key for economic growth
Economic growth is key for innovation
For economic growth innovation is key
Key for growth economic is innovation
questions.solution: Innovation is key for economic growth

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PERSEVERANCE:
questions.solution: Indolence

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The human brain is a complex organ. Q: It contains billions of neurons that transmit signals. R: Different regions control different functions. S: Neuroplasticity allows the brain to adapt and reorganise.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To give the benefit of the doubt':
questions.solution: To assume someone is honest

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She lost not only her wallet but also.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She lost her wallet and also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ to the needs of the poor.
questions.solution: indifferent

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I would rather prefer tea than coffee.
options: I would rather tea than coffee.
I would rather have tea than coffee.
I would rather prefer tea than coffee.
I prefer tea rather than coffee.
questions.solution: I would rather have tea than coffee.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The scientist ____ the experiment with great precision.
questions.solution: conducted

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PERIPHERAL:
questions.solution: Outer

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of OBSTINATE:
questions.solution: Flexible

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aeromagnetic survey: reduction to pole (RTP):
options: Recalculates anomalies as if measurement was at magnetic pole
questions.solution: RTP: removes asymmetry of magnetic anomalies. Anomalies centered over sources. Requires IGRF removal.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Turbidite Bouma sequence division B:
questions.solution: Bouma B: upper parallel lamination. Upper flow regime. Graded bedding often present. Part of complete turbidite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gondwana Supergroup:
options: Permian to Jurassic. Glacial (Talchir), Coal (Barakar, Raniganj), Plant fossils (Glossopteris)
questions.solution: Gondwana: rifted basins. Damodar, Son, Mahanadi, Godavari valleys. Major coal in India.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Yardang is an erosional feature carved by:
questions.solution: Yardang: streamlined ridge carved by wind. Aligned with prevailing wind. Found in arid regions with fine-grained rock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geophysical method — GPR:
options: Ground penetrating radar. High resolution, shallow (<10m)
questions.solution: GPR: electromagnetic waves. Utility detection, void mapping, archaeology.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: S-C mylonite fabric:
options: S (schistosity, strain) and C (shear, cisaillement) planes. Acute angle points to shear direction
questions.solution: S-C: S = elongate grains. C = discrete shear bands. C' (extensional shear bands) also.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cirque:
options: Armchair-shaped depression at head of glacial valley. Tarn = lake in cirque
questions.solution: Cirque: rotational slip, frost wedging. Headwall erosion. Bergschrund crevasse.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pegmatite zonation:
options: Border (fine) → Wall (intermediate) → Intermediate (coarse) → Core (quartz core). Rare-element enrichment inward
questions.solution: Pegmatite: LCT (Li-Cs-Ta) and NYF (Nb-Y-F) families. Giant crystals up to 14m (spodumene).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Step-drawdown test:
options: Well loss from incremental pumping rates. C·Q² term
questions.solution: Step: s = BQ + CQ². B: formation loss. CQ²: well loss. Well efficiency = BQ/(BQ+CQ²).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral's tendency to break along smooth planes related to its atomic structure is its:
questions.solution: Cleavage reflects planes of weak bonding in the crystal lattice.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Unconformity:
options: Gap in geologic record. Disconformity, angular unconformity, nonconformity
questions.solution: Angular: tilted older rocks below horizontal younger. Nonconformity: sedimentary on igneous/metamorphic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Isostasy is the principle that the lithosphere:
options: Is rigid and fixed in place
Floats in buoyant equilibrium on the denser mantle
questions.solution: Thicker/lighter crust rides higher; loading (ice, sediment) depresses it, like a floating raft.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Cleavage in mica is:
questions.solution: Mica: one direction of perfect cleavage. Splits into sheets.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: U-series dating: ²³⁰Th/²³⁴U disequilibrium:
questions.solution: ²³⁴U decays to ²³⁰Th. Disequilibrium → age. ²³⁰Th/²³⁴U = 1-e^{-λt}. Isochron method.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Liquefaction potential is highest in:
questions.solution: Liquefaction: saturated loose sand loses strength during shaking. High water table + loose sand + earthquake = high risk.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well logging — density (FDC):
options: Formation bulk density ρ_b from Compton scattering. Porosity: ϕ = (ρ_ma - ρ_b)/(ρ_ma - ρ_f)
questions.solution: Density: Cs¹³⁷ source. ρ_ma: sandstone 2.65, limestone 2.71, dolomite 2.87 g/cm³.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Glacial varves consist of couplets of:
questions.solution: Varves: annual glacial lake deposits. Summer: coarser meltout. Winter: fine clay settling under ice. Used for dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Walther's law of facies:
options: Vertically adjacent facies in a conformable succession were laterally adjacent in deposition
questions.solution: Walther: facies change vertically = lateral facies changes. Unconformities violate the law.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Boudinage structure indicates:
questions.solution: Boudinage: layer stretched and broken into sausage-shaped blocks. Indicates extension along layering. Common in metamorphic rocks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ophitic texture:
options: Plagioclase laths enclosed in pyroxene. 'Fabric' of dolerite
questions.solution: Ophitic: common in dolerite/gabbro. Subophitic: partial enclosure. Poikilitic: small grains in large.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: P-T-t path — prograde vs retrograde:
options: Clockwise (thickening → erosion): Barrovian. Counterclockwise (magmatic underplating): Buchan
questions.solution: P-T-t: prograde (burial + heating), retrograde (exhumation + cooling). Zonation in porphyroblasts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Digital Elevation Model (DEM):
options: Raster grid of elevation. Slope, aspect, hillshade derived from DEM
questions.solution: DEM: SRTM 30m, ASTER GDEM, ALOS 30m. Used for hydrology, landslide, viewshed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cement types in sandstone — paragenesis:
options: Early (calcite, pyrite) → Mesogenetic (quartz overgrowth, illite, ankerite) → Telogenetic (kaolinite, Fe-oxides)
questions.solution: Paragenesis: diagenetic sequence. Relative timing from cross-cutting and texture.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: (U-Th)/He dating of apatite:
options: He diffusion: closure temperature ~70°C. For exhumation studies
questions.solution: AHe: low-T thermochronometer. (U-Th)/He age = time since cooling through ~70°C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Partial melting:
options: Incomplete melting of source rock. First melt is more felsic
questions.solution: Partial melting of mantle → basaltic magma. Partial melting of crust → granitic melt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pleochroism in biotite:
options: Strong — dark brown to pale yellow (brown mineral)
questions.solution: Biotite: strong pleochroism. One cleavage direction. 'Bird's eye' extinction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granite is best classified as a(n):
questions.solution: Granite cools slowly at depth, producing large crystals; it is felsic and intrusive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ammonite suture line evolution:
options: Goniatitic (simple, Devonian) -> Ceratitic (moderate, Triassic) -> Ammonitic (complex, Jurassic-Cretaceous)
questions.solution: Suture complexity increases through time. Goniatitic: simple lobes. Ceratitic: tooth-like. Ammonitic: foliate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cataclastic metamorphism:
questions.solution: Cataclastic: crushing, grinding. Mylonite: fine-grained, foliated. Fault gouge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rock mass classification — Q-system:
questions.solution: Q: 6 parameters. Rating for tunnel support. Q < 0.01 (extremely poor) to Q > 400 (extremely good).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mineral color in thin section:
options: Pleochroism — color varies with crystallographic orientation
questions.solution: Pleochroism seen in biotite, hornblende, tourmaline. Property in plane-polarized light.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Artesian well:
options: Water rises above aquifer (confined aquifer under pressure). Flowing if > ground surface
questions.solution: Artesian: confined aquifer with potentiometric surface above ground. Flowing artesian.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal washing (beneficiation):
options: Gravity separation, flotation, dense medium. Reduces ash and sulfur
questions.solution: Washing: removes mineral matter (ash). Dense medium cyclone: main method. Float-and-sink test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 30 m of overburden above a 5 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 30/5 = 6 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 30 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 5 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Proximate analysis of coal gives:
options: Moisture, volatile matter, fixed carbon, ash
questions.solution: Proximate: moisture, VM, FC, ash (by difference). Ultimate: C, H, O, N, S.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 10 m³ of coal, 60 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 60/10 = 6 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 30% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+30+15) = 51%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about porosity and permeability is correct?
options: Sand has lower porosity than shale always
Clay can have high porosity but low permeability
High porosity always means high permeability
Permeability is independent of pore connectivity
questions.solution: Clay's tiny, poorly connected pores hold much water yet transmit it slowly — high porosity, low permeability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Garnet group — almandine:
questions.solution: Almandine: common in schists and gneisses. Deep red to brown.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Talik:
options: Unfrozen ground within permafrost. Open (through) or closed (isolation). Suprapermafrost, intrapermafrost, subpermafrost
questions.solution: Talik: groundwater flow thru permafrost. Open talik = talk connection to sub-permafrost water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Topology in GIS:
options: Spatial relationships: connectivity, adjacency, containment. Essential for network analysis
questions.solution: Topology: polygon topology (left/right polygons). Network: roads, rivers. Planar enforcement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slope stability: factor of safety FS = resisting forces / driving forces. FS > 1:
questions.solution: FS > 1: stable. FS = 1: marginally stable. FS < 1: failure. Design: FS > 1.3-1.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ignimbrite (welded tuff):
options: Pyroclastic flow deposit. Welded: glass shards fused at high temperature
questions.solution: Ignimbrite: welded pyroclastic. High temperature (>600°C) → welding. Devitrification of glass shards.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Palaeogene-Neogene boundary (India):
options: Around 23-24 Ma. Oligocene-Miocene transition. Siwalik start ~18 Ma
questions.solution: Paleogene-Neogene: Kutch and Assam basins (marine). Himalayan foreland basin (Siwalik).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary environments:
options: Continental (fluvial, aeolian, glacial), Transitional (delta, beach, lagoon), Marine (shelf, deep sea)
questions.solution: Each environment produces diagnostic sedimentary structures and facies.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kyanite:
options: High-P polymorph of Al2SiO5. Bladed. Blueschist to amphibolite facies
questions.solution: Kyanite: high pressure. Blue color. Hardness varies (4.5 parallel to 7 perpendicular to c-axis).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electromagnetic (EM) methods:
options: Time-domain (TDEM) and frequency-domain (FDEM). Conductive overburden → good for groundwater
questions.solution: EM: skin depth δ = 503√(ρ/f). Transient: airborne EM (AEM). VTEM, SkyTEM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carlin-type gold deposit:
options: Disseminated, sediment-hosted, arsenian pyrite. Nevada (USA). Low-grade, large-tonnage
questions.solution: Carlin: invisible gold in arsenian pyrite. Carbonate host. Sediment-hosted disseminated gold.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 25% of its original parent isotope (half-life 4.47 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 25% remaining ⇒ 2 half-lives ⇒ age = 2×4.47 = 8.94 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fold types by hinge shape:
questions.solution: Chevron: straight limbs. Box: flat top. Ptygmatic: complex, incompetent layers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 5% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 8% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (5+25+8) = 62%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater recharge:
options: Natural (rainfall infiltration) and artificial (recharge wells, ponds, check dams)
questions.solution: Recharge: ~10-20% of rainfall in alluvial areas. Artificial: managed aquifer recharge (MAR).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Specific gravity of native gold:
questions.solution: Gold (Au) — SG 15-19. Heavy mineral. Placer deposits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: V-shaped valley:
options: Fluvial incision (youthful stage). Steep sides, narrow floor
questions.solution: V-shaped: downcutting > lateral erosion. Rapids and waterfalls common.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spaceborne sensors — Sentinel-2:
options: MSI (13 bands, 10-60m). 5-day revisit. 290km swath. Vegetation, agriculture

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rip-rap for erosion protection:
options: Hard, durable rock (granite, basalt). Angular, well-graded. Weight: W₅₀ design
questions.solution: Riprap: properly sized. Bedding layer underneath. Filter layer prevents piping.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pegmatite:
options: Very coarse-grained igneous rock (>2.5 cm crystals). Enriched in rare elements
questions.solution: Pegmatite: high volatile content (H2O, F, Cl). Source of Li, Be, Cs, rare earths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kurnool Group:
options: Overlies Cuddapah unconformably. Diamictite, limestone, quartzite. Neoproterozoic
questions.solution: Kurnool: Banganapalle quartzite (diamond-bearing). Owk shale. Narji limestone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phosphorite:
options: Sedimentary rock >50% phosphate minerals (apatite). Upwelling zones
questions.solution: Phosphorite: marine sediments, Peru-Chile, Morocco. Phosphorus fertilizer. Guano.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Serpentinite:
options: Alteration of ultramafic rock (olivine→serpentine). Slip planes — landslide hazard
questions.solution: Serpentinite: tectonic melanges. Asbestos minerals (chrysotile). Weak, slippery.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) frequency affects:
questions.solution: GPR: higher freq = better resolution but less depth. Lower freq = more depth but less resolution. 10 MHz - 2 GHz.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 5 Mt has an average metal grade of 3%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 5 Mt × 3% = 150 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 30% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+30+15) = 47%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Polymorphism:
options: Same composition, different crystal structure (e.g., graphite/diamond, calcite/aragonite)
questions.solution: Polymorphs: diamond (cubic) and graphite (hexagonal) — both C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermal inertia:
options: Resistance to temperature change. High for wet/dense materials. Day-night ΔT for mapping
questions.solution: Thermal inertia: √(ρ·k·C_p). Quartz: high. Water: very high. Dry sand: low. Used in geology.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rare earth element (REE) patterns: chondrite-normalized. LREE enrichment (La/Sm)_N > 1 indicates:
options: Enriched mantle source or crustal contamination
questions.solution: LREE enriched: OIB, arc, continental. LREE depleted: N-MORB. Chondrite normalized: divides by chondrite values.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic grade indicators in pelites:
options: Chlorite → biotite → garnet → staurolite → kyanite → sillimanite (increasing grade)
questions.solution: Barrovian index minerals: each stable in specific P-T window. Increasing grade = higher T/P.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 5 km², is 3 m thick, with density 1.4 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 5×10⁶ m²×3 m×1.4 t/m³ ≈ 21 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GPS positioning: code-phase accuracy vs carrier-phase:
questions.solution: Carrier-phase: millimeter precision. RTK: real-time kinematic. Static: post-processed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Iron-rich sedimentary rocks:
options: Banded Iron Formation (BIF): Precambrian, chert + iron oxides (hematite, magnetite)
questions.solution: BIF: major Fe ore. Algoma-type (volcanic) vs Superior-type (cratonic). Banded: microbands.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Karst — tower karst (fengcong):
options: Steep-sided limestone towers separated by plains. Peak cluster
questions.solution: Tower karst: Guilin (China), Ha Long Bay. Fenglin: isolated towers. Fengcong: connected peaks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chert and radiolarite:
options: Deep marine siliceous ooze. Radiolaria, diatoms, sponge spicules. Recrystallized to microcrystalline quartz
questions.solution: Chert: SiO₂. Radiolarite: radiolarian tests. Bedded or nodular chert in limestone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pediment:
options: Gently sloping erosional surface at mountain base. Bedrock with thin alluvium
questions.solution: Pediment: desert piedmont. Pediplain: coalescing pediments. Parallel retreat of slopes (Penck).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Mohs hardness, which comparison is correct?
questions.solution: A harder mineral scratches a softer one; quartz (7) > feldspar (6).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stream power law: Ω = ρgQS. Incision rate E = K·Ω^m·S^n. For m=1, n=1:
questions.solution: Stream power: erosion rate depends on discharge and slope. m≈1, n≈1 (simplified.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mohr circle for stress: center = (σ₁+σ₃)/2, radius = (σ₁-σ₃)/2. For σ₁=100MPa, σ₃=40MPa:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stereonet: β-axis of fold determined by:
options: Intersection of great circles through two fold limbs
questions.solution: β-axis: intersection of two great circles representing limb orientations. π-diagram: poles to limbs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Storativity S:
options: Volume released per unit area per unit head drop. Confined: elastic storage (~10⁻⁵-10⁻³)
questions.solution: S = Ss·b (specific storage × thickness). Unconfined: S ~ S_y (specific yield, 0.1-0.3).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+35+20) = 37%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 100 m on flat ground and dips at 20°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 100×sin20° ≈ 34.2 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Muscovite belongs to:
questions.solution: Muscovite — white mica. Good electrical insulator. Used in capacitors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 50 m/day) of cross-section 500 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 1 km², is 3 m thick, with density 1.4 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 1×10⁶ m²×3 m×1.4 t/m³ ≈ 4.2 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Radiometric dating works because a radioactive parent isotope decays to a daughter at a rate set by its:
questions.solution: Decay is exponential with a fixed half-life, so the parent/daughter ratio gives an absolute age.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 200 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granulite:
options: High-grade metamorphic. No hydrous minerals (pyroxene + feldspar + garnet)
questions.solution: Granulite: dry metamorphism. H2O absent. Pyroxene stable, biotite unstable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m seam over 6 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.75. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 6M m²×3×1.4×0.75 ≈ 18.9 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chalk:
questions.solution: Chalk: White Cliffs of Dover. Pure CaCO3. Used in cement, lime.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Pencil cleavage forms when:
options: Two cleavages intersect at high angle
questions.solution: Pencil cleavage: intersection of two cleavages at ~90°. Rock breaks into pencil-shaped fragments. Common in fold hinges.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+25+20) = 47%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquitard:
options: Low permeability unit (silt, clay, shale). Poor yield, may store/transmit slowly
questions.solution: Aquitard: retards vertical flow. Leakage through aquitard → aquitard-aquifer system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral that effervesces (fizzes) when dilute hydrochloric acid is applied is:
questions.solution: Calcite (CaCO₃) reacts with dilute HCl, releasing CO₂ — a standard field test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Laccolith:
options: Concordant intrusion that domes overlying strata
questions.solution: Laccolith: mushroom-shaped. Overlying beds arched upward.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Moho discontinuity depth:
options: ~5-10 km (oceanic), ~30-50 km (continental). Base of crust. Seismic P-wave jump ~6.7→8.1 km/s
questions.solution: Moho: crust-mantle boundary. Defined by Vp. Faster in mafic lower crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquiclude:
options: Impermeable unit (clay, massive shale). No significant yield
questions.solution: Aquiclude: prevents flow. Confining layer in confined aquifer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lithostratigraphy:
options: Rock units based on lithology (Formation, Member, Group)
questions.solution: Formation: mappable unit. Member: subdivision. Group: several formations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Uranium deposits — unconformity-related:
options: Athabasca Basin (Canada). High grade (>1% U₃O₈). Basement + sandstone. U/Pb age ~1.5 Ga
questions.solution: Unconformity: massive U within altered basement. Cigar Lake, McArthur River.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geological Time Scale:
options: Precambrian (88%), Paleozoic (545-251 Ma), Mesozoic (251-66 Ma), Cenozoic (66 Ma-present)
questions.solution: Hadean, Archean, Proterozoic (Precambrian). Phanerozoic: Paleo-Meso-Cenozoic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetotelluric (MT) method:
options: Natural EM field (lightning, solar wind). Measures impedance Z = E/H
questions.solution: MT: deep crustal imaging. Low frequency → deep. Resistivity model. MTZ: mid-crustal conductor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of corundum on Mohs scale:
questions.solution: Corundum (Al2O3) — 9. Sapphire and ruby are gem varieties.
